intro: Abel-shittim (meaning meadow of the acacias), commonly called simply Shittim, was a location in the plains of Moab east of the Jordan, nearly opposite Jericho. It served as the forty-second and final encampment of Israel before crossing into Canaan. There Israel sinned with the daughters of Moab (Numbers 25), and from there Joshua sent the two spies to Jericho (Joshua 2:1). The prophet Micah invokes the memory of Shittim in recalling God's saving acts from Egypt to Canaan.
